summary: I read `guides/backend-guide.md` and `guides/ui-style-guide.md`, then audited the iteration-3 "Live pipeline status summary" diff against them — the `Pipelines::StatusSummary` PORO, `BroadcastStatus`/`BroadcastColumn` services, `Phases::Advance`/`Approve`/`ManagerTick`, the pipelines and approvals controllers, the `_status_summary`/`_phase_column`/`index`/`show` views, the `StatusHelper`/`PipelinesHelper` tone tables, and the `Pipeline` model. I confirmed all three findings from the prior guide-alignment iteration are now fixed (Advance owns its transaction; ManagerTick/Advance broadcasts are deferred with `after_all_transactions_commit`; the gate button/input now use the sanctioned component classes), and that the core is well-aligned: a pure derivation PORO correctly homed in `app/lib`, a single shared tone table so dot and badge can't diverge, smallest-DOM-unit broadcasts, load-time truth with streams as enhancement, and an `aria-live` region where the sentence (not color) carries meaning. I emitted a **needs_work** verdict with one major finding — the human gate-approval path (`ApprovalsController → Phases::Approve → Phases::Advance`) changes pipeline status without ever calling `Pipelines::BroadcastStatus`, so the summary goes stale for non-approving viewers, violating the UI guide's "Live by default" — plus three minor findings (business branching via `pipeline.reload.completed?` in `ApprovalsController`, a stray `tmp_run_tests.rb` committed at the repo root, and an off-palette `text-gray-700` in the compact summary row), each citing the specific guide rule. The verdict is written to `.pipeliner/phases/04-review/main/guide-alignment-critic/verdict.json`.
